title: You are a structured-output adapter for Cognee.
body_text: You are a structured-output adapter for Cognee. Return ONLY one valid JSON object. No markdown fences, no commentary. The JSON MUST validate against this Pydantic JSON Schema for SummarizedContent: { "description": "Class for a single class label summary and description.", "properties": { "summary": { "title": "Summary", "type": "string" }, "description": { "title": "Description", "type": "string" } }, "required": [ "summary", "description" ], "title": "SummarizedContent", "type": "object" } System instructions from Cognee: You are a top-tier summarization engine. Your task is to summarize text and make it versatile. Be brief and concise, but keep the important information and the subject. Use synonim words where possible in order to change the wording but keep the meaning. Input text: output/cognee_codex_full_bundle/nodes.jsonl I’m reading `output/cognee_codex_full_bundle/nodes.jsonl` to extract the source text and produce a schema-valid concise summary. The file is a JSONL knowledge-node dump with research entities, claims, evidence spans, and performance notes drawn from papers, repos, and social feeds. I’m checking its composition so the summary reflects the dataset rather than

commands_run: jq -r '.metadata.source_path // "NULL"' output/cognee_codex_full_bundle/nodes.jsonl | sed 's#/[^/]*$##' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | sed -n '1,30p'
